title: fix(text-splitters): restore lazy imports for heavy optional dependencies
body_text:
## Summary

- Moves `nltk`, `spacy`, `sentence-transformers`, and `konlpy` imports back inside class constructors/functions so they are only loaded when the respective splitter is actually instantiated
- Adds a subprocess-based regression test to verify no heavy packages are imported at `langchain_text_splitters` load time

## Why

PR #32325 moved these optional dependency imports to module-level `try/except` blocks (to satisfy ruff's `PLC0415` rule). Since `__init__.py` imports all four splitter modules, this caused `import langchain_text_splitters` to eagerly load all optional heavy packages, resulting in:

- A PyTorch NVML warning (`UserWarning: Can't initialize NVML`) on non-GPU machines
- A ~650MB memory spike on import (74MB → 736MB), vs ~50MB in 0.3.x

The fix restores the lazy import pattern with `# noqa: PLC0415` to suppress the linter rule, which is the correct trade-off when a dependency has high instantiation cost.

## Review notes

- The `PLC0415` suppressions are intentional — these are optional heavy dependencies that should never be loaded unless the user explicitly instantiates the splitter class
- The regression test uses a subprocess for proper isolation (the test file itself imports `langchain_text_splitters` at the top, so `sys.modules` checks within the same process would not reflect a clean import state)

Fixes #35437.

> **AI disclaimer:** This PR was developed with assistance from Claude Code (Anthropic AI).

entities.name: NLTK
entities.description: A natural language toolkit, identified as an optional heavy dependency.

entities.name: SpaCy
entities.description: A natural language processing library, identified as an optional heavy dependency.

entities.name: Sentence-Transformers
entities.description: A library for sentence embeddings, identified as an optional heavy dependency.

entities.name: Konlpy
entities.description: A Korean natural language processing library, identified as an optional heavy dependency.

entities.name: Langchain Text Splitters
entities.description: A module within Langchain that provides text splitting functionalities and uses optional heavy dependencies.

entities.name: PR #32325
entities.description: A previous Pull Request that introduced eager loading of optional dependencies in `langchain_text_splitters`.

entities.name: Eager Loading
entities.description: The issue of optional heavy packages being loaded immediately upon module import, leading to performance problems.

entities.name: PyTorch NVML Warning
entities.description: A warning related to NVML initialization that occurs on non-GPU machines due to eager loading.

entities.name: Memory Spike
entities.description: A significant increase in memory usage upon importing `langchain_text_splitters` caused by eager loading.

entities.name: Lazy Imports
entities.description: A pattern where dependencies are loaded only when needed, not at module import time, used to fix the eager loading issue.

entities.name: PLC0415
entities.description: A ruff linter rule that was previously satisfied by module-level imports, but is now suppressed for lazy imports.

entities.name: Regression Test
entities.description: A test added to verify that heavy packages are not imported at `langchain_text_splitters` load time.

entities.name: Subprocess
entities.description: A method used by the regression test for proper isolation.

extracted_data.summary: This issue fixes eager loading of heavy optional dependencies like NLTK and SpaCy in `langchain_text_splitters`, which was introduced by PR #32325 and caused memory spikes and NVML warnings, by restoring lazy imports and adding a regression test.
